summary: Add the set-scope `membership` roster predicate — a declared field on each matched artifact must lie in the feature-set drawn from a second matched set, not a static `enum`.

per.section: The set scope (the roster) — predicates over a matched set

edit.description: Add a `Membership` declaration (the constrained field F on the role's matched set S₁, plus a source clause naming S₂: an artifact kind, a `match` selector, and the source feature G) and an optional `membership` field on `Role`; parse it off the `[role.<name>]` table (mirroring `parse_count`/`parse_unique`) with a `RoleBadMembership` malformation error variant. Keep it orthogonal to `count`/`unique`/`required`.

Decide membership in `check`: for each role carrying a `membership` clause, build the allowed set from feature G over the S₂-matching artifacts (the `by_kind` map already in scope carries every kind, so S₂ may be a different kind — no signature change), then emit one `Diagnostic::error` per S₁ filler whose field-F scalar value is absent from that set. Add a `ROLE_MEMBERSHIP_RULE` id and a helper mirroring `duplicates`/`out_of_band`, with unit tests; a filler missing F carries no value to check and is skipped.

tests.asserts: An imported corpus with a `membership` role fires when a filler's field value is outside the S₂-derived set and is clean when every filler's value is drawn from it; unit coverage of the parse + decide lives in `src/compose.rs`/`src/roster.rs`.

acceptance: `temper check` over an imported corpus runs a `membership` roster clause — flagging every matched artifact whose declared field falls outside the set derived from the second selector's matched artifacts, and staying silent when all are members.
notes: Two-selector: S₁ is the role's own `match`; S₂ is a second selector + kind + source feature G — the allowed set is corpus-*derived*, unlike the static field `enum`. `roster::check` already holds the full `by_kind` map, so an S₂ over another kind needs no signature change. Disjoint from parked SPEC-KIND-GATE (main.rs). Follow-on: `(typed-reference)`, where S₂ is 'kind K conforming-to contract C'.

summary: Dispatch `check` over specs: embed `contracts/spec.toml`, project `ws.specs` via `spec_features`, and run admissibility + `engine::validate` (the advisory `max_lines` clause) over the spec features.

per.section: Worked example: `spec`, temper's own custom kind

gate.reason: the untracked `contracts/spec.toml` is curated human territory (build embeds it via `include_str!` but never writes/adds it); a human must commit it before the gate can embed it in a clean commit

edit.description: Add `BUILTIN_SPEC_CONTRACT = include_str!("../contracts/spec.toml")`; in the `Check` arm project `ws.specs` to spec features via `extract::spec_features`, parse the spec contract, and extend the diagnostic stream with `engine::admissibility` + `engine::validate` over them.

tests.asserts: `temper check` over an imported corpus runs the spec contract — the over-length specs trip `max_lines` at advisory severity and the run still exits 0 absent `--deny-advisories`.

acceptance: `temper check` over an imported corpus runs the spec contract — flagging the over-length specs at advisory severity and exiting 0 (non-blocking) absent `--deny-advisories`.
notes: Build-order: needs SPEC-FEATURES (the extractor, shipped — `extract::spec_features` on disk) plus a human to commit the untracked `contracts/spec.toml`. Runs only spec.toml's active `max_lines` clause; its two commented clauses (decisions-name-alternatives, references-resolve) await `(decision-marker-predicate)` and the references-resolve frontier — both also downstream of that spec.toml commit.
